work.reflection: Taking on a leadership role gave me the space to put years of observation into practice. Figuring out how to build a scalable architecture from scratch has been a satisfying challenge, and it feels incredibly rewarding to be laying the tracks myself.
details: Leading the backend effort, building Go services focused on keeping things simple, scalable, and manageable by a small team.
Built AWS infrastructure for services across Splitting Point's Roblox titles.
Working with an AI-assisted development workflow - not generating code blindly, but using AI as a thinking partner alongside my own judgement.

work.reflection: Operating in a space where the software itself is the core product shifted my perspective on engineering. Getting to navigate the sheer scale of those systems slowly deepened my understanding of what it takes to keep a massive platform running smoothly.
details: Maintained and extended legacy IIS applications in C#, reverse-engineering undocumented workflows to ship features without breaking things.
Helped design the replacement platform in modern .NET while keeping the legacy system running.
On-call for production incidents across systems outside my squad's scope.

work.reflection: Transitioning from university into a large corporate environment taught me how to align technical work with actual business needs. Learning to view code as a mechanism for delivering value, rather than just an academic exercise, fundamentally changed the way I evaluate solutions.
details: Maintained a distributed TypeScript microservices ecosystem across a team of 10-20 developers.
Built integrations with third-party vendors and legacy platforms, including data pipelines to clean and transform incoming datasets.

work.reflection: Building a tool that was going to be used by other developers gave me my first taste of real-world engineering. Watching experienced teams turn complex problems into shipping features showed me a rhythm to the work that I found completely addictive.
details: Designed, developed, and published raygun4reactnative - a React Native library for crash reporting and real-user monitoring.

interests.summary: Upgrading old handhelds and getting into the retro community.
